scenario: ⚽ Sports and Fandom — Asking about a score: 'What's the score?'
text:
🎧 YouGlish
To ask about the status of a live game, ask: {{c1::"What's the score of the game? Who's winning?"}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'wʌ·sðə skɔːr əv ðə geɪm?'
Rules applied:
• What's the → /wʌ·sðə/ (linking final /s/ of 'what's' to 'the' + schwa reduction)
• score of → /skɔː·rəv/ (linking C+V: final /r/ of 'score' links to 'of' /əv/)

🧠 'What's the score?' is the universal question for sports updates. Note how 'of the' reduces to /əv ðə/ where 'of' is a mere murmur.
usage:
• "What's the score? Is Arsenal still down?"
  • "What's the score of the game? I missed the first half."
spanish: '¿Cómo va el partido? ¿Quién va ganando?' — Preguntar por el marcador. Fonética: 'What's the' suena '/wʌ-sðə/' y 'score of' se une de forma que la R de 'score' viaja al sonido schwa de 'of'.

scenario: ⚽ Sports and Fandom — Rooting for a team: 'Root for...'
text:
🎧 YouGlish
To ask someone which team they support, use the verb 'root': {{c1::"Who do you root for in the Premier League?"}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'huː də jə ruːt fər...'
• do you → /də·jə/ (schwa reduction)
• root for → /ruːt fər/ (schwa reduction on 'for')

🧠 'Root for' is the primary American phrasal verb meaning 'to support / support a team'. British English equivalents are 'support' or 'follow'.
usage:
• "I root for the Lakers, but my brother is a Celtics fan."
  • "Who do you root for in the NFL?"
spanish: '¿A quién le vas en la Premier League?' — 'Root for' es la expresión típica en inglés americano para indicar a qué equipo apoyas (el equivalente británico es 'support'). Fonéticamente, 'do you' y 'for' se reducen a schwa.
